steps.title: Identify the source of the underestimation
steps.content: The team concludes previous assessments underestimated capacity. The decisive clue: alkaline extraction (which removes wax) recovered 53.8%, while water recovered only 7.6%—revealing that particles accumulate in wax that basic extraction methods cannot reach.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content: Choice A directly supports this: if prior studies used extraction methods that don't affect wax (as A states), they would have measured much less than the true amount, explaining the underestimation. Choices B, C, and D address tree diversity, concentrations exceeding 53.8%, and treatment order—factors unrelated to the extraction-method limitation. That matches choice A — A is correct.
